body.label: topology_7.root.thm
body.claim: Let $X$ be an uncountable set equipped with the co-countable topology, where $U$ is open iff $U=\emptyset$ or $X\setminus U$ is countable. A sequence $(x_n)$ in $X$ converges to $L\in X$ if and only if there exists an integer $N$ such that for all $n\ge N$, $x_n=L$.

proof_steps.claim: Let $X$ be an uncountable set equipped with the co-countable topology, where $U$ is open iff $U=\emptyset$ or $X\setminus U$ is countable. A sequence $(x_n)$ in $X$ converges to $L\in X$ if and only if there exists an integer $N$ such that for all $n\ge N$, $x_n=L$.
